text: Charles Coverdale. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Coverdale C (2026). readecb: Access European Central Bank Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readecb.
code: @Manual{,   title = {readecb: Access European Central Bank Data},   author = {Charles Coverdale},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readecb}, }

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: R package accessing data published European Central Bank (ECB) via ECB Data Portal API.

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: European Central Bank central bank euro area. sets monetary policy 20 countries use euro - importantly, key interest rates determine cost borrowing across eurozone. also publishes vast quantity statistical data covering exchange rates, inflation, money supply, lending conditions, government bond yields. data freely available ECB’s Statistical Data Warehouse. problem accessing programmatically requires knowledge SDMX series keys - opaque strings like FM/D.U2.EUR.4F.KR.MRR_RT.LEV encode dataflow, frequency, area, currency, instrument dimensions. package abstracts away entirely.

what: How is readecb different from the ecb package?
title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: existing ecb package provides general-purpose interface ECB data, works well already know SDMX series key need. readecb complements providing named convenience functions commonly used datasets - can call ecb_policy_rates() ecb_hicp() without needing look key structures. also caches results locally uses lightweight dependency stack (httr2 + cli).

title: Access European Central Bank Data
code: install.packages("readecb")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/readecb")

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: download functions accept date parameters cache = TRUE default.

what: 1. What has the ECB done to interest rates since the inflation shock?
title: Access European Central Bank Data
code: library(readecb)  # Returns daily data with one row per rate per day rates <- ecb_policy_rates(from = "2022-01") head(rates) #>         date                     rate value #>   2022-01-03  Deposit facility rate  -0.50 #>   2022-01-03  Main refinancing rate   0.00 #>   2022-01-03  Marginal lending rate   0.25 #>   2022-01-04  Deposit facility rate  -0.50 #>   2022-01-04  Main refinancing rate   0.00 #>   2022-01-04  Marginal lending rate   0.25

what: 2. How has the euro moved against the dollar?
title: Access European Central Bank Data
code: fx <- ecb_exchange_rate("USD", from = "2020-01") head(fx) #>         date currency    value #>   2020-01-01      USD 1.121500 #>   2020-02-01      USD 1.091400 #>   ...  # Compare multiple currencies fx_multi <- ecb_exchange_rate(c("USD", "GBP", "JPY"), from = "2024-01")

title: Access European Central Bank Data
code: # Euro area aggregate (U2) by default hicp <- ecb_hicp(from = "2020-01") tail(hicp, 3) #>         date country value #>   2025-06-01      U2   2.0 #>   2025-07-01      U2   2.2 #>   2025-08-01      U2   2.2  # Compare across countries hicp_countries <- ecb_hicp(c("DE", "FR", "IT", "ES"), from = "2024-01") head(hicp_countries) #>         date country value #>   2024-01-01      DE   3.1 #>   2024-01-01      ES   3.5 #>   2024-01-01      FR   3.4 #>   2024-01-01      IT   0.9 #>   2024-02-01      DE   2.7 #>   2024-02-01      ES   2.9

title: Access European Central Bank Data
code: # Euro area aggregate by default mortgages <- ecb_mortgage_rates(from = "2020-01") head(mortgages) #>         date country value #>   2020-01-01      I9  1.41 #>   2020-02-01      I9  1.41 #>   2020-03-01      I9  1.39 #>   2020-04-01      I9  1.38 #>   2020-05-01      I9  1.36 #>   2020-06-01      I9  1.39

what: 5. What is the 10-year government bond yield?
title: Access European Central Bank Data
Access European Central Bank Data
text: named functions cover popular datasets, ECB publishes 100 dataflows. need something niche - payment statistics, securities issuance, bank lending surveys - can use ecb_get() dataflow name SDMX series key. Use list_ecb_dataflows() browse ’s available.
code: df <- ecb_get("EXR", "M.USD.EUR.SP00.A", from = "2024-01")  flows <- list_ecb_dataflows() head(flows)

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: Data cached locally tools::R_user_dir("readecb", "cache") first download. Subsequent calls parameters return instantly cache. force fresh download, pass cache = FALSE. clear entire cache, call clear_cache().

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: package part family R packages economic fiscal data. share consistent interface - named functions, tidy data frames, local caching - designed work together.

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: Please report bugs requests https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readecb/issues.

title: Access European Central Bank Data
text: European Central Bank, ECB, euro area, interest rates, EURIBOR, ESTR, HICP, inflation, exchange rates, yield curve, money supply, GDP, unemployment, government debt, monetary policy, R package

title: Clear the readecb cache — clear_cache
text: Deletes locally cached ECB data files. next call data function re-download ECB Data Portal.

title: Clear the readecb cache — clear_cache
code: clear_cache()

title: Clear the readecb cache — clear_cache
text: Invisible NULL.

title: Clear the readecb cache — clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ clear_cache() #> ℹ No cache to clear. # }

title: Euro short-term rate (ESTR) — ecb_estr
text: Returns euro short-term rate, ECB's benchmark overnight interest rate replaced EONIA.

title: Euro short-term rate (ESTR) — ecb_estr
code: ecb_estr(from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Euro short-term rate (ESTR) — ecb_estr
text: Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Euro short-term rate (ESTR) — ecb_estr
text: data frame columns: date Date. value Numeric. Rate percent.

title: EURIBOR interbank lending rates — ecb_euribor
text: Returns EURIBOR (Euro Interbank Offered Rate) one tenors. EURIBOR benchmark rate euro area banks lend , widely used reference rate mortgages, loans, derivatives.

title: EURIBOR interbank lending rates — ecb_euribor
code: ecb_euribor(tenor = "3M", from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: EURIBOR interbank lending rates — ecb_euribor
text: tenor One "1M" (1 month), "3M" (default), "6M", "12M". Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: EURIBOR interbank lending rates — ecb_euribor
text: data frame columns: date Date. tenor Character. EURIBOR maturity. value Numeric. Rate percent per annum.

title: Euro exchange rates — ecb_exchange_rate
text: Returns ECB reference exchange rates one currencies euro.

title: Euro exchange rates — ecb_exchange_rate
code: ecb_exchange_rate(   currency = "USD",   frequency = c("monthly", "daily"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Euro exchange rates — ecb_exchange_rate
text: currency Character vector ISO 4217 currency codes (e.g. "USD", "GBP"). Use list_exchange_rates() see available currencies. frequency One "monthly" (default) "daily". Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Euro exchange rates — ecb_exchange_rate
text: data frame columns: date Date. currency Character. ISO currency code. value Numeric. Units foreign currency per euro.

title: Euro area GDP — ecb_gdp
text: Returns quarterly real GDP euro area ECB's national accounts dataset (MNA).

title: Euro area GDP — ecb_gdp
code: ecb_gdp(from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Euro area GDP — ecb_gdp
text: Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Euro area GDP — ecb_gdp
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day quarter. value Numeric. GDP millions euros (chain-linked volumes).

title: Fetch any ECB dataflow — ecb_get
text: generic fetcher direct access ECB's dataflows. Use list_ecb_dataflows() discover available dataflows.

title: Fetch any ECB dataflow — ecb_get
code: ecb_get(dataflow, key, from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Fetch any ECB dataflow — ecb_get
text: dataflow Character. dataflow identifier (e.g. "EXR", "FM"). key Character. SDMX dimension key (e.g. "M.USD.EUR.SP00."). Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Fetch any ECB dataflow — ecb_get
text: data frame. Columns vary dataflow always include TIME_PERIOD OBS_VALUE.

what: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt
title: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt
text: Returns annual general government consolidated gross debt percentage GDP euro area, ECB's government finance statistics dataset (GFS).

title: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt
code: ecb_government_debt(from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt
text: Optional start date (year, e.g. "2000"). Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt
text: data frame columns: date Date. 1 January year. value Numeric. Government debt percentage GDP.

title: Euro area government debt-to-GDP ratio — ecb_government_debt

what: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp
title: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp
text: Returns HICP inflation data one euro area countries country groups. default euro area aggregate ("U2").

title: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp
code: ecb_hicp(   country = "U2",   measure = c("annual_rate", "index", "monthly_rate"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp
text: country Character vector country codes. Use "U2" euro area aggregate, ISO 2-letter codes "DE" (Germany), "FR" (France), "" (Italy), "ES" (Spain). measure One : "annual_rate" (default) – year--year percentage change "index" – index level (2015 = 100) "monthly_rate" – month--month percentage change Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp
text: data frame columns: date Date. country Character. Country area code. value Numeric. Inflation rate index level.

title: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) — ecb_hicp

what: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
title: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
text: Returns composite cost--borrowing indicator loans non-financial corporations euro area specific country.

title: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
code: ecb_lending_rates(country = "U2", from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
text: country Character. Country code: "U2" euro area aggregate (default), ISO 2-letter code "DE", "FR", "", "ES". Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
text: data frame columns: date Date. country Character. Country area code. value Numeric. Interest rate percent per annum.

title: Lending interest rates to non-financial corporations — ecb_lending_rates
Euro area monetary aggregates — ecb_money_supply
text: Returns outstanding amounts M1, M2, M3 monetary aggregates euro area.

title: Euro area monetary aggregates — ecb_money_supply
code: ecb_money_supply(   aggregate = c("M3", "M2", "M1"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Euro area monetary aggregates — ecb_money_supply
text: aggregate One "M3" (default), "M2", "M1". Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Euro area monetary aggregates — ecb_money_supply
text: data frame columns: date Date. value Numeric. Outstanding amount millions euros.

title: Mortgage interest rates — ecb_mortgage_rates
text: Returns composite cost--borrowing indicator house purchase loans euro area specific country.

title: Mortgage interest rates — ecb_mortgage_rates
code: ecb_mortgage_rates(country = "U2", from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Mortgage interest rates — ecb_mortgage_rates
text: country Character. Country code: "U2" euro area aggregate (default), ISO 2-letter code "DE", "FR", "", "ES". Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Mortgage interest rates — ecb_mortgage_rates
text: data frame columns: date Date. country Character. Country area code. value Numeric. Interest rate percent per annum.

title: ECB policy interest rates — ecb_policy_rates
text: Returns three main ECB policy rates: main refinancing operations rate, deposit facility rate, marginal lending facility rate.

title: ECB policy interest rates — ecb_policy_rates
code: ecb_policy_rates(from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: ECB policy interest rates — ecb_policy_rates
text: Optional start date (e.g. "2020" "2020-01"). Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: ECB policy interest rates — ecb_policy_rates
text: data frame columns: date Date. rate Character. One "Main refinancing rate", "Deposit facility rate", "Marginal lending rate". value Numeric. Rate percent per annum.

title: Euro area unemployment rate — ecb_unemployment
text: Returns monthly harmonised unemployment rate euro area ECB's labour force statistics dataset (LFSI).

code: ecb_unemployment(from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

text: Optional start date. Optional end date. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

text: data frame columns: date Date. value Numeric. Unemployment rate percentage.

what: readecb: Access European Central Bank Data — readecb-package
title: readecb: Access European Central Bank Data — readecb-package
text: Provides clean, tidy access statistical data published European Central Bank (ECB) via ECB Data Portal API https://data.ecb.europa.eu. Covers policy interest rates, EURIBOR, euro exchange rates, harmonised consumer price inflation (HICP), euro area yield curves, euro short-term rate (ESTR), monetary aggregates (M1, M2, M3), mortgage lending rates, GDP, unemployment, government debt--GDP. dataset dedicated function abstracts away underlying SDMX key structure, users need know series codes. generic fetcher also provided direct access ECB's 100-plus dataflows. Data downloaded first use cached locally subsequent calls.

title: readecb: Access European Central Bank Data — readecb-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

title: readecb 0.1.0
text: Initial release. ecb_policy_rates(), ecb_estr(), ecb_mortgage_rates(), ecb_lending_rates() ECB interest rate data. ecb_euribor() EURIBOR interbank lending rates 1M, 3M, 6M, 12M tenors. ecb_hicp() harmonised consumer price inflation. ecb_exchange_rate() list_exchange_rates() euro reference exchange rates. ecb_yield_curve() euro area government bond yields. ecb_money_supply() M1, M2, M3 monetary aggregates. ecb_gdp() quarterly euro area real GDP. ecb_unemployment() monthly euro area unemployment rate. ecb_government_debt() annual government debt--GDP ratio. ecb_get() list_ecb_dataflows() direct access ECB dataflow. clear_cache() remove locally cached files. Data sourced ECB Data Portal API https://data.ecb.europa.eu.
